title: Vivid Motions
description: Dynamic Animations & Immersive Actions to Minecraft.
body:
![Logo](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/408d80375aafeac6baf45f74c566377faa2740d8.png)

# Vivid Motions: Java Edition
Every move feels real
Vivid Motions makes Minecraft Java feel alive.
Your character moves smooth. Tools swing with weight. Eating looks real. Mobs act alive. Everything just feels better.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/O5ZAqSQ4r7Q" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## What it does:

- You move naturally — no stiff arms or legs
- Items in your hand look 3D and move when you do
- Mobs walk, bounce, and react like real animals
- Lights are smooth, like in optifine

## Steps to install:
<details>
<summary>Modrinth App [Recommended]</summary>

1. Open the Modrinth App and log in if needed.
2. Click the "+" (Add Instance) button in the top-left.
3. Select "Import Modpack" from the dropdown.
4. Browse your files and select the .mrpack file.
5. Choose your Minecraft version (if prompted) and click "Import."
6. Once done, launch the new instance from the main list.

</details>

>_The launchers below require a zip file, then an .mrpack.
Please use an online converter for them._

<details>
<summary>Curseforge App</summary>

1. Open the CurseForge App > Minecraft tab.
2. Click "Create Custom Profile" (top-right, next to the search bar).
3. In the pop-up, select "Import" > Choose "From .zip file".
4. Browse to your .zip (e.g., VividMotions.zip) > Select it.
5. Name the profile (e.g., "Vivid Motions") > Click "Create".
6. Review mods in the profile editor if needed (e.g., tweak configs) > Hit "Play"

</details>
<details>
<summary>Prism Launcher</summary>

1. Open Prism Launcher.
2. Click "Add Instance" (or right-click in the instances list > Create Instance).
3. In the dialog, go to the "Import from zip" tab.
4. Paste the URL to the .mrpack (if online) or click the folder icon to select your local .mrpack file.
5. Select the Minecraft version if prompted, then click "OK" to import.
6. Edit mods/configs if needed, then launch.

</details>
<details>
<summary>ATLauncher</summary>

1. Open ATLauncher.
2. Go to the "Packs" tab on the left sidebar.
3. Click "Import" at the bottom of the packs list.
4. Select "Local Pack" or "From File," then browse and choose your .mrpack file.
5. ATLauncher will detect it as a modpack—name the instance and click "Import."
6. Wait for download/extraction, then launch from the instances list.

</details>
<details>
<summary>MultiMC / PolyMC</summary>

1. Open MultiMC (or PolyMC fork).
2. Click "Add Instance" > Select "Import from zip."
3. Enter the local path to your .mrpack file.
4. MultiMC will parse the .mrpack—confirm Minecraft version and click "OK."
5. The instance appears in the list—edit jars/mods if prompted, then launch.

</details>
</details>
<details>
<summary>Other Launchers(Buttons may vary)</summary>

1. Convert .mrpack to .zip
2. Extract the .zip file
3. Open %appdata% and go to .minecraft
4. Drag the files in the Resource pack folder from the .zip to the Resource pack folder in .minecraft
5. Drag the files in the Mods folder from the .zip to the Mods folder in .minecraft
6. Open your launcher and create a new instance
7. Select your version and click "Play"


</details>
Download Vivid Motions now and feel the difference.
